summaries.150192: The issue is confirmed reproducible on Linux master and beta channels but not consistently reproduced by the Flutter team member on their Linux setup. Bisection by the reporter narrowed the regression to Flutter 3.19.0; keyboard events work under Wayland but not X11 on KDE Neon. A workaround using HardwareKeyboard or Focus widget instead of FocusableActionDetector was shared, but FocusableActionDetector specifically does not trigger shortcuts on Linux while Focus does, narrowing the bug to the shortcuts/actions layer inside FocusableActionDetector. Currently open and stalled, with no PR or root cause pinned.
summaries.95553: Confirmed web-only regression introduced between Flutter 2.5.3 (working) and 2.8.x. After submitting via the Enter key, the TextField retains focus visually but stops accepting keyboard input; clicking a submit button instead avoids the bug because focus briefly moves. A workaround of wrapping requestFocus in a Future.delayed(10ms) was shared and confirmed effective for some users. Some users report the issue tied to the index.html bootstrap script and switching to the async engineInitializer.autoStart() pattern resolved it for them. Issue status is unclear — a team member noted they could not reproduce it in a later test with the original code sample.
summaries.99050: Affects Windows touch-screen devices (specifically Surface tablets and other Windows devices with touch capability) where the on-screen keyboard pops up on every touch after a TextField has once been focused. The root cause identified in comments is that when a TextField is disposed or focus is lost, the engine still holds an active IME/input connection on Windows, causing the OS touch keyboard to appear on any subsequent touch. Multiple workarounds involve placing an autofocus transparent ElevatedButton on every screen to redirect focus; FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.nextFocus() also works as a workaround while unfocus() does not. Issue is over two years old with no engine-level fix landed.
summaries.124483: Root cause identified in comments: both Flutter and the underlying <textarea> element update the selection simultaneously on scroll, causing the cursor to jump to the pre-scroll tap position rather than the current one. A fix for Flutter Web on Android/Chrome was merged via engine PR #41202, resolving cursor placement on Android Chrome. The iOS/Safari fix remained outstanding and was tracked as part of a larger effort at issue #120613; as of the latest comments, long-press with the magnifier is the only reliable cursor placement mechanism on iOS. Issue is partially fixed (Android) and still open for iOS.
summaries.102084: Confirmed reproducible on iPad (not iPhone) on stable and master channels; the issue is specific to scrolling vertically on a focused TextField when keyboardDismissBehavior is set to ScrollViewKeyboardDismissBehavior.onDrag. A commenter identified the likely cause as a pointer event not being released, leaving the engine believing two active pointers exist and causing the scroll/gesture system to lock. Two PRs partially fixed the issue but it remained reproducible on iPad simulator after they landed. Still reproducible as of stable 3.19.5 and master; assignee was eventually removed after no status updates.
summaries.139054: The severe 4-second animation delay seen on iPhone 7 (iOS 15.7.9) was no longer reproducible on newer stable/master after the original report, though a residual jerk on the chat input view when the keyboard appears remains. A commenter identified the fundamental root cause: Flutter receives keyboard height changes via events (viewInsets) but lacks frame-by-frame timing information from the OS animation, so layout updates are out of sync with the native keyboard animation curve. A detailed proposal for engine-side per-frame keyboard height updates via CADisplayLink (iOS) and WindowInsetsAnimation.Callback (Android) was posted. Issue remains open and was re-pinged to @LongCatIsLooong.
summaries.92349: Regression identified as introduced between Flutter 2.5.3 (working) and 2.6.x (broken) on Flutter Web/Android. A commenter bisected the root cause to PR #86312 (autofill changes), where autofill hints cause the keyboard to re-show after backgrounding and returning to the app; removing autofill hints from the TextField avoids the bug. The issue was routed to the web team. Workarounds include switching to stable 2.5.3, using Get.offAll to clear the widget tree, or using unfocus with proper autofill configuration.
summaries.133576: Confirmed Windows-only; macOS does not reproduce it. A commenter identified that the bug occurs specifically with CRLF (Windows-style) line endings in pasted text — pasting text with Unix LF line endings works correctly. The cursor rendering is the buggy part: the cursor is rendered at the start of the text when it logically sits on a CRLF newline, but typing proceeds correctly from that position, indicating a visual rendering bug rather than a text-model bug. No PR was linked and the issue remains open for investigation.
summaries.171695: The resource leak reported in STANDARD_ERROR is caused by Surface objects in SurfaceTextureSurfaceProducer tests (SurfaceTextureSurfaceProducerTest) that are not released before the Robolectric test completes; the leak originates in a different test class than the ScribeChannelTest named in the title. A team member confirmed this and noted that test-level resource leaks are low severity since the test world is destroyed regularly, but flagged that it could indicate a production-code leak if the same code path runs in production. No PR linked; status is open.
summaries.112354: A team member confirmed that the scroll view fails to scroll to keep a WebView input field visible when the Flutter TextField does not have focus at the moment the WebView keyboard is invoked; if the Flutter TextField is focused first, the scroll view does correctly adjust. The issue is attributed to Flutter not being able to detect the WebView's input focus changes to trigger scroll adjustment. The issue was triaged as a limitation of Flutter not controlling WebView focus, with the suggestion to use JavaScript to blur the WebView input on FocusNode focus changes. Workaround: set resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false.
summaries.181169: The crash is a failed assertion in EdgeDraggingAutoScroller._scroll triggered when the drag target (the TableView inside a CustomScrollView) is larger than the enclosing scrollable, which violates an invariant in the selection auto-scroll logic. Confirmed reproducible on both stable 3.38.x and master. The issue is labeled "waiting for PR to land (fixed)", indicating a fix has been prepared but not yet merged as of the time the issue was filed.
